title: Spicy Velvety Algerian Semolina Bowl
description: A warm, spicy semolina bowl with toasted pita cubes, perfect for a post‑workout pick‑me‑up.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a medium saucepan over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and sauté until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Stir in the harissa paste and paprika, cooking for 1 minute to release the spices.
Pour in the water, add salt, and bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Gradually whisk in the semolina, stirring continuously to avoid lumps.
Continue cooking and stirring until the mixture thickens and becomes velvety, about 6‑8 minutes.
Remove the pan from heat and stir in the lemon zest, cooked chickpeas, and chopped parsley.
Warm the toasted pita cubes in a dry skillet over medium heat until they are slightly crisp, 2‑3 minutes.
Serve the semolina mixture in bowls and top each with a handful of toasted pita cubes.
Optionally, squeeze fresh lemon juice or sprinkle extra parsley before serving.
